name: mac-letterhead
description: Create letterheaded PDFs from Markdown or merge existing PDFs with your letterhead template
long_description:
Mac Letterhead applies your organisation's letterhead PDF to documents with a single click — or through Claude.

Drop Markdown content and Claude will convert it to a professionally-formatted PDF, automatically detecting margins from your letterhead template. Or merge an existing PDF with your letterhead overlay.

**Setup:** Place your letterhead PDF at `~/.letterhead/<style>.pdf`. Optionally add a matching CSS file at `~/.letterhead/<style>.css` for custom typography.

**Features:**
- Markdown → letterheaded PDF via WeasyPrint (high-quality) or ReportLab (fallback)
- GitHub Flavored Markdown: tables, task lists, code highlighting
- Smart margin detection — analyses your letterhead to find safe printable areas
- Multi-page letterhead support (different first/even/odd page templates)
- Five blend strategies: darken (default), multiply, overlay, transparency, reverse

tools.name: create_letterhead_pdf
tools.description: Convert Markdown content to a letterheaded PDF

tools.name: merge_letterhead_pdf
tools.description: Apply a letterhead template to an existing PDF

tools.name: analyze_letterhead
tools.description: Analyse a letterhead template to determine margins and printable areas

style.title: Letterhead Style
style.description: Style name used to resolve ~/.letterhead/<style>.pdf (and optional ~/.letterhead/<style>.css). Leave blank for generic mode — style must then be provided per tool call.

output_directory.title: Output Directory
output_directory.description: Directory where generated PDFs are saved.
